description: The wagon: wheels made into a carrier — the farm's rolling barn, the road's beast of burden that eats nothing. Built on 'rolu' (wheel), the family doing its usual work: know the wheel, read the wagon.
sound_symbolism: 'rolu' turns twice at the front of the word and 'tha' beds it like a laden floor. Say it slowly and the axle creaks.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'kalora rolutha natu.' (The horse pulls the wagon.) 'lo miona roe rolutha napuro kolua.' (The people carry grain with the wagon.) Related: 'rolu' (wheel), 'kolua' (carry).

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The wagon moved whatever the season made — harvest, firewood, households — and market day was measured by what one wagon could hold.

semantic_domains.community: What moves the harvest to market
